concept: Person faces discrimination at work (race, gender, disability, age, etc.) and overcomes barriers through determination, skill, and persistence - breaking barriers and achieving success despite discrimination
emotionalArc: Struggle (discrimination/barriers) → perseverance (fighting back, proving worth) → breakthrough/transformation (overcoming discrimination, achieving success)
keyEmotionalMoments: Haben: The 'you're being left out' inner voice during class laughter
Haben: Being called 'inspiring' as a euphemism for pity
Haben: Learning to communicate through braille keyboard with classmates
Haben: First successful networking conversation at the bar
Haben: Guiding drunk friend Liqin home—roles reversed
Haben: The gibberish typing humor moment ('I'm already Deafblind. I don't want to be Deaf, blind, and drunk.')
Haben: Triumph - Skadden Fellowship and graduation
Dr. Ingram: Being called 'a dumb Black bitch' by a customer at age 17
Dr. Ingram: The racist joke on the board during Black History Month
Dr. Ingram: A kindergartner calling her boyfriend a racial slur
Dr. Ingram: Being accused of being in a 'gang' for her sorority photos
Dr. Ingram: Discovering massive pay inequity ($32K vs $45K)
Dr. Ingram: Being physically pushed by a professor
Dr. Ingram: Triumph - Founding her own companies to fight discrimination
Myrna: Father losing job, mother becoming fish vendor
Myrna: Working as domestic helper to pay for education
Myrna: Fear seeing massive construction equipment at 150cm tall
Myrna: Men telling her to 'go home and find a husband'
Myrna: Almost tipping equipment onto hazardous waste
Myrna: Triumph - Leading 250+ workers as supervisor
notes: Composite story from Haben Girma (deafblind Harvard Law), Dr. Cheryl Ingram (24-year discrimination journey), and Myrna Pitaluna-Alngog (construction industry). Focus on the journey of overcoming discrimination through determination, skill, and persistence.
